title: nardophyllum lanatum
intro: Nardophyllum lanatum, also known as the hairy nardoo or woolly nardoo, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the southern regions of Australia. The plant has been traditionally used by indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be used to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of Nardophyllum lanatum include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. Nardophyllum lanatum can be prepared as an herbal remedy through various methods, including infusion, decoction, and poultice, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for therapeutic purposes.
